title: [ENHANCEMENT] Add z-loss
body:
Summary:
- Introduces z-loss regularization for stabilizing training by constraining logits in the final layer.
- Loss now supports cross-entropy plus an optional z-loss term. A new coefficient controls the contribution of z-loss.
- Logging is extended to expose the three components: cross-entropy loss, z-loss, and the total loss.

What changes:
- Adds a z_loss_coef knob (default 0.0) and wiring to enable/disable z-loss during training.
- Computes z-loss from the final-layer logits and adds it to the primary loss when enabled.
- Logs cross-entropy loss, z-loss, and total loss for easier monitoring and debugging.

How to enable:
- Set z_loss_coef > 0 (via config, or environment variable MEGATRON_Z_LOSS_COEF) to activate z-loss regularization.
- The default behavior remains unchanged (no z-loss) to preserve existing training stability and performance.

Notes:
- This change is localized to the loss computation path and logging, minimizing impact on other parts of the training loop.
- Potential follow-ups: add unit tests for z-loss computation, expose z-loss specifics in docs, and verify FP16/TF32 precision stability.

step_plan.summary: Enhance loss computation with an optional z-loss regularization term and extended logging. Adds a configurable z_loss_coef knob (default 0.0) to activate z-loss and log cross-entropy, z-loss, and total loss while preserving current behavior when disabled.

steps.operation: Extend compute_loss to compute and incorporate z-loss with optional logging
steps.reason: Introduce a new z_loss_coef knob (default 0.0) to enable z-loss regularization without changing existing behavior; compute z-loss from final-layer logits and add to cross-entropy to form total_loss when enabled; extend logging to expose cross-entropy, z-loss, and total loss for monitoring
steps.side_effects: If z_loss_coef > 0, may affect gradients and training stability; requires updates to tests and docs; minor performance/logging overhead; downstream components consuming compute_loss outputs may need to accommodate the additional z_loss term and new logging fields
